Value: EFFECTS OF PYRUVATE SALTS, PYRUVIC ACID, AND BICARBONATE SALTS IN PREVENTING EXPERIMENTAL OXALATE UROLITHIASIS IN RATS
All the diets were fed in powder form, and the rats were allowed tap water ad libitum.

Data Field: Loss to Follow-up (n per arm if reported)
Baseline Stone Size or Burden (if reported)

Value: Calcium-oxalate lithogenic diet (a glycolic-acid diet) containing 3% glycolic acid

Data Field: Outcome A: Stone Recurrence / New Stone Formation (DICHOTOMOUS)

Value: heavy calculi deposits in all six rats

Data Field: Withdrawals Due to Adverse Events (n per arm)
Data Requires Imputation for SD? (Yes / No / Not sure)

Value: Body-weight gain was significantly increased in the pyruvate and bicarbonate groups compared to the glycolic-acid group, except for pyruvic acid which was similar to control.
